headline: 142% Phone Penetration But Only 58% Digital Literacy
context: Malaysia smartphone penetration reached 142% in 2025 but digital literacy shows only 58% of adults proficient.

cards.big: 142% smartphone penetration but only 58% digital literacy among adults
cards.sub: Device access outpaces ability to use technology safely and productively

cards.big: Adults over 55 have 35% literacy compared to 82% for aged 18-34
cards.sub: Only 22% of seniors can identify phishing putting them at highest scam risk

cards.big: Rural digital literacy at 42% lags urban 68% by 26 points
cards.sub: Programmes reach only 180,000 of 3.5 million identified underserved adults

cards.big: Government spent RM85 million on digital literacy programmes in 2025
cards.sub: Budget covers only 5% of the underserved population

cards.big: Literacy gaps test whether digital access without digital skills creates new vulnerabilities
142% phone penetration but 58% digital literacy. Access without capability produces consumption, not empowerment.
